shapes.name: Text 5
shapes.text:
The Framework for Building
LLM-Powered Applications

shapes.name: Text 3
shapes.text: LangChainis a Python and JavaScript framework that simplifies the creation of applications powered by Large Language Models. It provides modular building blocks—chains, agents, memory, and tools—that developers can compose into sophisticated AI workflows.

shapes.name: Text 7
shapes.text: Created by Harrison Chase as a simple tool-connection framework

shapes.name: Text 11
shapes.text: 30,000+ new users monthly; 43% of organizations use LangGraph in production

shapes.name: Text 15
shapes.text: From prototyping to production deployment with full lifecycle support

shapes.name: Text 3
shapes.text: Four complementary tools forming a complete toolkit for modern LLM application development

shapes.name: Text 9
shapes.text: Build modular LLM workflows using prompts, models, tools, and memory. Best for prototyping and linear applications with simple APIs.

shapes.name: Text 17
shapes.text: Manage complex, stateful workflows with branching, looping, and persistence. Built on LangChain components for production-grade agents.

shapes.name: Text 25
shapes.text: Trace, evaluate, and monitor LLM applications with detailed insights. Framework-agnostic debugging and performance tracking.

shapes.name: Text 33
shapes.text: Drag-and-drop interface for rapid prototyping and stakeholder collaboration. Export flows to production-ready LangChain code.

shapes.name: Text 3
shapes.text: How LangChain's components connect to form complete AI applications

shapes.name: Text 8
shapes.text: LLMs, Chat Models, and Embedding models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Hugging Face, and 100+ providers

shapes.name: Text 13
shapes.text: Manage and customize prompts with dynamic variables, few-shot examples, and structured formatting

shapes.name: Text 18
shapes.text: Sequential workflows combining multiple components—simple chains for single LLM calls, multi-step chains for complex pipelines

shapes.name: Text 23
shapes.text: Autonomous systems that reason about problems, decide which tools to use, and take actions dynamically

shapes.name: Text 28
shapes.text: Preserve conversation context with buffer, window, summary, and custom memory types

shapes.name: Text 33
shapes.text: Store and search embeddings for semantic similarity—integrates with Pinecone, Chroma, Weaviate, FAISS

shapes.name: Text 38
shapes.text: External capabilities (APIs, databases, search) and data ingestion (PDFs, web pages, databases)

shapes.name: Text 42
shapes.text: Input → Document Processing → Embedding & Storage → Retrieval → Generation (with Tools/Agents) → Output

shapes.name: Text 3
shapes.text: Compose chains with the pipe operator for readable, maintainable workflows

shapes.name: Text 8
shapes.text:
# Define components
template ="""Question: {question}
Answer: Let's think step by step."""
prompt = PromptTemplate.from_template(template)
output_parser = StrOutputParser()
# Compose with LCEL pipe operator
chain = prompt | llm | output_parser
# Execute
response = chain.invoke({
"question":"How many apples?"
})

shapes.name: Text 14
shapes.text: "You started with five apples, removed two by throwing them away and then consumed one more, which leaves you with two apples. So, the final answer is you have two apples left."

shapes.name: Text 15
shapes.text: The LLM applies step-by-step reasoning automatically through prompt engineering

shapes.name: Text 21
shapes.text: Get tokens as they're generated for responsive UIs

shapes.name: Text 31
shapes.text: Run independent steps simultaneously for efficiency

shapes.name: Text 36
shapes.text: Reuse and nest chains like building blocks

shapes.name: Text 40
shapes.text: The|operator passes output from one component as input to the next, creating clean, readable data pipelines that mirror Unix philosophy

shapes.name: Text 3
shapes.text: Ground LLM outputs in external knowledge to reduce hallucinations and enable domain-specific applications

shapes.name: Text 41
shapes.text: "Instead of relying solely on the LLM's internal parameters, the model queries an external retriever, searches through a corpus, and conditions its generation on retrieved documents—grounding output in concrete, up-to-date data."

shapes.name: Text 46
shapes.text: Ground responses in real data rather than model parameters, dramatically improving factual accuracy

shapes.name: Text 50
shapes.text: Update knowledge bases without retraining expensive models—simply add new documents

shapes.name: Text 54
shapes.text: Enable LLMs to answer questions about private or specialized data not in training sets

shapes.name: Text 58
shapes.text: Trace responses back to source documents for transparency and verification

shapes.name: Text 60
shapes.text: Supported Vector Stores:Pinecone, Chroma, Weaviate, FAISS, Qdrant, Milvus, pgvector, and 20+ more

shapes.name: Text 3
shapes.text: Enable LLMs to reason, decide, and execute actions dynamically

shapes.name: Text 8
shapes.text:
# Define tools with Pydantic schemasclassWeatherInput(BaseModel):
location:str= Field(...)
unit:str="celsius"
weather_tool = StructuredTool.from_function(
func=get_weather,
name="get_weather",
args_schema=WeatherInput
)
# Create ReAct agent with memory
agent = create_react_agent({
"llm": llm,
"tools": [weather_tool, search_tool],
"checkpointSaver": memory
})

shapes.name: Text 12
shapes.text: Reasoning +Acting: The agent thinks through problems step-by-step, decides which tools to use, executes them, and observes results before continuing.

shapes.name: Text 29
shapes.text: Retrieve current information not in training data, verify facts, research topics dynamically

shapes.name: Text 33
shapes.text: Execute SQL, query APIs, access enterprise systems for real-time data retrieval

shapes.name: Text 37
shapes.text: Perform precise mathematical operations, data analysis, and statistical computations

shapes.name: Text 41
shapes.text: Run Python, JavaScript, or shell commands to solve programming tasks

shapes.name: Text 45
shapes.text: Set max_iterations to prevent infinite loops, implement human-in-the-loop checkpoints for sensitive operations, and validate tool outputs before use

shapes.name: Text 3
shapes.text: Maintain context across interactions for coherent, multi-turn conversations

shapes.name: Text 9
shapes.text: Stores the entire conversation without modification. Simplest and most intuitive approach—preserves exact context but can grow large for long conversations.

shapes.name: Text 11
shapes.text: Best for:Short conversations where exact history matters

shapes.name: Text 17
shapes.text: Keeps only the last k interactions (e.g., last 4 exchanges). Drops older messages to manage token usage. FIFO queue behavior—oldest messages removed first.

shapes.name: Text 19
shapes.text: Best for:Ongoing conversations where recent context matters most

shapes.name: Text 25
shapes.text: Uses an LLM to create concise summaries of conversations. Incrementally updates as new messages arrive. Preserves key information while dramatically reducing tokens.

shapes.name: Text 27
shapes.text: Best for:Long conversations where key points matter more than exact words

shapes.name: Text 33
shapes.text: Combines approaches—keeps recent messages in full detail while summarizing older ones. Smart threshold triggers summarization when token limit exceeded.

shapes.name: Text 35
shapes.text: Best for:Technical discussions needing both detail and history

shapes.name: Text 39
shapes.text: The recommended approach for production applications—automatic history management, session support for multiple users, clean LCEL integration, and future-proof architecture

shapes.name: Text 3
shapes.text: Clear differentiation between LangChain, LangGraph, and LangSmith

shapes.name: Text 7
shapes.text: UseLangChainwhen your workflow is linear or you're prototyping

shapes.name: Text 11
shapes.text: Move toLangGraphwhen you need branching, retries, or persistence

shapes.name: Text 15
shapes.text: LayerLangSmithwhen testing, debugging, or moving to production

shapes.name: Text 3
shapes.text: Enterprise applications delivering measurable business value

shapes.name: Text 9
shapes.text: Unified access to siloed corporate knowledge across document stores, knowledge bases, and employee expertise. Optimized chunking and metadata enrichment contextualize data.

shapes.name: Text 20
shapes.text: Automated loan processing with multi-step verification, compliance checks, and human-in-the-loop approvals. LangChain orchestrates automated and manual components.

shapes.name: Text 31
shapes.text: RAG system combining internal and external regulatory sources with automated change monitoring. Complete audit trails via LangSmith for compliance documentation.

shapes.name: Text 42
shapes.text: LangGraph coordinates research, coding, and validation agents in customer-facing workflows. Agents debate citations, self-correct, and escalate to humans when confidence is low.

shapes.name: Text 44
shapes.text: Key Features:State persistence, human checkpoints, retry logic, parallel execution

shapes.name: Text 97
shapes.text: Switch between providers without rewriting application code. The same chain works with OpenAI, Anthropic, or local models—experiment freely and optimize for cost, latency, or quality.

shapes.name: Text 8
shapes.text: Begin withLangChainfor linear workflows—chatbots, Q&A systems, RAG pipelines

shapes.name: Text 12
shapes.text: Move toLangGraphwhen you need branching, retries, persistence, or multi-agent coordination

shapes.name: Text 16
shapes.text: LayerLangSmithfrom day one for observability, debugging, and production monitoring

shapes.name: Text 18
shapes.text: "The future of AI development lies in intelligent automation—and LangChain provides the infrastructure to build it at scale."
